paper_title: Ensemble Kalman filter based sequential Monte Carlo sampler for sequential Bayesian inference
paper_claims: In sequential Bayesian parameter estimation with additive Gaussian observation noise, defining the SMCS forward kernel via the Ensemble Kalman filter as a Gaussian centered at the previous particle plus a Kalman-gain–weighted innovation with covariance given by propagated observation noise (with small jitter), and pairing it with a Gaussian approximation to the optimal backward kernel fitted from the previous ensemble, yields a tractable sampler whose proposal marginal approximates the current posterior whenever the previous proposal approximates the previous posterior.

An adaptive weight refinement scheme that uses inexpensive approximate weights based on Gaussian surrogates and triggers exact weight computation via an effective-sample-size threshold reduces forward model evaluations while preserving exact importance weights at refinement steps.

source_questions.claim: In sequential Bayesian parameter estimation with additive Gaussian observation noise, defining the SMCS forward kernel via the Ensemble Kalman filter as a Gaussian centered at the previous particle plus a Kalman-gain–weighted innovation with covariance given by propagated observation noise (with small jitter), and pairing it with a Gaussian approximation to the optimal backward kernel fitted from the previous ensemble, yields a tractable sampler whose proposal marginal approximates the current posterior whenever the previous proposal approximates the previous posterior.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): In sequential Bayesian parameter estimation with additive Gaussian observation noise, a tractable sampler is obtained by defining the SMCS forward kernel via the Ensemble Kalman filter as a Gaussian centered at the previous particle plus a Kalman-gain–weighted innovation with covariance given by propagated observation noise with small jitter and pairing it with a Gaussian approximation to the optimal backward kernel fitted from the previous ensemble, which combination below reflects this construction and its consequence that the proposal marginal approximates the current posterior whenever the previous proposal approximates the previous posterior? [A] Forward kernel: EnKF-based Gaussian with Kalman-gain–weighted innovation and propagated observation-noise covariance plus small jitter; backward kernel: Gaussian approximation to the optimal fitted from the previous ensemble; consequence: proposal marginal approximates the current posterior conditional on the previous proposal approximating the previous posterior. [B] Forward kernel: Laplace distribution centered at zero with fixed covariance; backward kernel: none; consequence: proposal marginal approximates the current posterior regardless of previous-stage quality. [C] Forward kernel: bootstrap particle filter transition with resampling only; backward kernel: uniform; consequence: proposal marginal matches the current posterior only asymptotically in infinite ensembles. [D] Forward kernel: EnKF-based Gaussian using prior covariance and no jitter; backward kernel: exact optimal kernel assumed known; consequence: proposal marginal equals the current posterior in all cases.

source_questions.claim: In sequential Bayesian parameter estimation with additive Gaussian observation noise, defining the SMCS forward kernel via the Ensemble Kalman filter as a Gaussian centered at the previous particle plus a Kalman-gain–weighted innovation with covariance given by propagated observation noise (with small jitter), and pairing it with a Gaussian approximation to the optimal backward kernel fitted from the previous ensemble, yields a tractable sampler whose proposal marginal approximates the current posterior whenever the previous proposal approximates the previous posterior.

[A] Always, regardless of the quality of the previous proposal. [B] Only when the observation noise is heavy-tailed and non-Gaussian. [C] Whenever the previous proposal approximates the previous posterior. [D] Only in exactly linear-Gaussian models with an exact Kalman filter.

source_questions.claim: In sequential Bayesian parameter estimation with additive Gaussian observation noise, defining the SMCS forward kernel via the Ensemble Kalman filter as a Gaussian centered at the previous particle plus a Kalman-gain–weighted innovation with covariance given by propagated observation noise (with small jitter), and pairing it with a Gaussian approximation to the optimal backward kernel fitted from the previous ensemble, yields a tractable sampler whose proposal marginal approximates the current posterior whenever the previous proposal approximates the previous posterior.

source_questions.question: Assertion (A): In sequential Bayesian parameter estimation with additive Gaussian observation noise, defining the SMCS forward kernel via the Ensemble Kalman filter as a Gaussian centered at the previous particle plus a Kalman-gain–weighted innovation with covariance given by propagated observation noise with small jitter and pairing it with a Gaussian approximation to the optimal backward kernel fitted from the previous ensemble yields a tractable sampler whose proposal marginal approximates the current posterior whenever the previous proposal approximates the previous posterior. Reason (R): The EnKF-based Gaussian forward kernel with Kalman-gain–weighted innovations and propagated observation-noise covariance, together with a Gaussian backward kernel fitted from the previous ensemble, keeps computations tractable and propagates approximation quality from one step to the next.

Reason (R): The forward kernel is independent of the previous particle and the backward kernel is exact rather than fitted.

paper_title: Equilibrium: an elasticity controller for parallel tree search in the cloud
paper_claims: Maintaining a user-defined target efficiency for elastic parallel tree search in the cloud is achieved by monitoring workload efficiency—a runtime measure of the fraction of essential computation that approximates elastic efficiency—and dynamically scaling processing units to meet this target, which thereby enforces a constant cost-per-problem-size ratio across runs.

source_questions.claim: Maintaining a user-defined target efficiency for elastic parallel tree search in the cloud is achieved by monitoring workload efficiency—a runtime measure of the fraction of essential computation that approximates elastic efficiency—and dynamically scaling processing units to meet this target, which thereby enforces a constant cost-per-problem-size ratio across runs.

[A] By monitoring workload efficiency—a runtime measure of the fraction of essential computation that approximates elastic efficiency—and dynamically scaling processing units to meet the target, enforcing a constant cost-per-problem-size ratio across runs [B] By fixing processing units and monitoring network latency, ensuring a constant throughput across runs [C] By monitoring memory utilization and scaling storage capacity, enforcing a constant runtime regardless of problem size [D] By periodically checkpointing and statically allocating resources, guaranteeing constant energy use per run

paper_title: Using autoencoders for session-based job recommendations
paper_claims: Learning latent representations of anonymous job-browsing sessions with autoencoder architectures (classical, denoising, and variational) from interaction data alone or combined with content features and using these embeddings for k-nearest-neighbor retrieval yields session-based job recommendations that maintain high coverage, achieve competitive accuracy, and increase system- and session-level novelty relative to established baselines.

source_questions.claim: Learning latent representations of anonymous job-browsing sessions with autoencoder architectures (classical, denoising, and variational) from interaction data alone or combined with content features and using these embeddings for k-nearest-neighbor retrieval yields session-based job recommendations that maintain high coverage, achieve competitive accuracy, and increase system- and session-level novelty relative to established baselines.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which statement fully captures the approach and outcomes for session-based job recommendation? [A] Learning latent representations of anonymous job-browsing sessions with classical, denoising, and variational autoencoder architectures from interaction data alone or combined with content features, and using the resulting embeddings for k-nearest-neighbor retrieval, yields session-based job recommendations that maintain high coverage, achieve competitive accuracy, and increase system- and session-level novelty relative to established baselines. [B] Using supervised sequence classifiers on labeled job applications with profile features and ranking by cosine similarity produces session-based job recommendations that sacrifice coverage for higher accuracy and reduce novelty compared to established baselines. [C] Training matrix factorization on explicit ratings and deploying beam search retrieval produces session-based job recommendations with state-of-the-art accuracy but lower coverage and unchanged novelty relative to established baselines. [D] Employing graph neural networks on content features only, with exact-match retrieval, results in session-based job recommendations with maximal coverage but decreased accuracy and novelty relative to established baselines.

source_questions.claim: Learning latent representations of anonymous job-browsing sessions with autoencoder architectures (classical, denoising, and variational) from interaction data alone or combined with content features and using these embeddings for k-nearest-neighbor retrieval yields session-based job recommendations that maintain high coverage, achieve competitive accuracy, and increase system- and session-level novelty relative to established baselines.

[C] Learning latent session representations with classical, denoising, and variational autoencoders from interaction data alone or combined with content features, and using those embeddings for k-nearest-neighbor retrieval, yields recommendations that maintain high coverage, achieve competitive accuracy, and increase both system- and session-level novelty compared to established baselines. paper_title: Human segmentation in surveillance video with deep learning
paper_claims: A deep encoder–decoder convolutional network trained on composited images of green-screen-captured human shapes over site-specific background frames can automatically segment people in surveillance videos without requiring a static camera, constrained subject motion, or auxiliary annotations such as pose or contours.

Applying a bilateral filter to synthetic training composites to attenuate high-frequency foreground–background edge discrepancies improves generalization to real indoor and outdoor images by better matching natural boundary transitions.
Inverse-frequency weighting of classes in the cross-entropy loss effectively mitigates foreground–background imbalance and enhances foreground pixel classification.

An architecture with a VGG-16–initialized encoder and a decoder that upsamples via unpooling using stored pooling indices enables memory-efficient dense pixel classification suitable for video surveillance streams.

[C] When trained on composited images of green-screen-captured human shapes over arbitrary stock backgrounds, it can segment people but requires pose and contour annotations to function. [D] When trained end-to-end on unlabeled surveillance footage, it can segment people provided that subject motion is constrained and the camera remains static.

paper_title: A deep-learning model for urban traffic flow prediction with traffic events mined from twitter
paper_claims: Data-level fusion of traffic measurements, weather variables, and geo-specific Twitter signals about traffic events—filtered to reliable road-traffic organization accounts—within a deep bidirectional LSTM stacked autoencoder improves multi-step short-term urban traffic flow prediction accuracy relative to traffic-only approaches, particularly under non-recurring disruptions.

[C] It improves only single-step long-term predictions and shows no benefit during non-recurring disruptions. [D] It degrades accuracy because social media noise overwhelms the model even when accounts are filtered.

paper_title: Fast and Accurate Edge Computing Energy Modeling and DVFS Implementation in GEM5 Using System Call Emulation Mode
paper_claims: An OS-independent DVFS capability in GEM5’s system call emulation mode, triggered by application-issued pseudo-instructions that switch among predefined CPU clock domains, combined with an architecture-agnostic energy model that decomposes dynamic and leakage power and parameterizes effective capacitance by workload class calibrated from hardware measurements—while accounting for DVFS transition energy, sleep modes, and systematic offset corrections—enables fast, accurate energy estimation for microcontroller-class edge systems using only GEM5 instruction-mix and activity statistics.

source_questions.claim: An OS-independent DVFS capability in GEM5’s system call emulation mode, triggered by application-issued pseudo-instructions that switch among predefined CPU clock domains, combined with an architecture-agnostic energy model that decomposes dynamic and leakage power and parameterizes effective capacitance by workload class calibrated from hardware measurements—while accounting for DVFS transition energy, sleep modes, and systematic offset corrections—enables fast, accurate energy estimation for microcontroller-class edge systems using only GEM5 instruction-mix and activity statistics.

paper_title: Bayesian estimation and likelihood-based comparison of agent-based volatility models
paper_claims: Bayesian estimation via Hamiltonian Monte Carlo of continuous-state approximations to agent-based financial market models enables rigorous parameter inference and predictive likelihood-based comparison, with mechanisms allowing persistent mispricing delivering predictive performance comparable to stochastic volatility benchmarks while exhibiting complex posterior geometries and limited parameter identifiability.

source_questions.claim: Bayesian estimation via Hamiltonian Monte Carlo of continuous-state approximations to agent-based financial market models enables rigorous parameter inference and predictive likelihood-based comparison, with mechanisms allowing persistent mispricing delivering predictive performance comparable to stochastic volatility benchmarks while exhibiting complex posterior geometries and limited parameter identifiability.

[A] It enables rigorous parameter inference and predictive likelihood-based comparison; mechanisms allowing persistent mispricing deliver predictive performance comparable to stochastic volatility benchmarks; and the posteriors exhibit complex geometries with limited parameter identifiability. [B] It fails to support predictive likelihood-based comparison, mispricing mechanisms perform worse than stochastic volatility benchmarks, and posteriors are simple with well-identified parameters. [C] It relies on variational inference rather than Hamiltonian Monte Carlo, mispricing must be eliminated to match stochastic volatility benchmarks, and posterior geometry is trivial with strong identifiability. [D] It only applies to discrete-state agent-based models, delivers descriptive rather than rigorous parameter inference, and achieves superior predictive performance to stochastic volatility benchmarks with unimodal, well-identified posteriors.

[C] Approximate Bayesian computation without predictive likelihoods, no reliable parameter inference, mispricing mechanisms lagging stochastic volatility benchmarks, and clear identifiability with simple posteriors. [D] Variational Bayes on discrete-state representations, comparable performance only without mispricing, and convex posteriors with uniquely identified parameters.

paper_title: Influenza-like illness prediction using a long short-term memory deep learning model with multiple open data sources
paper_claims: Using weekly regional data from Taiwan, a long short-term memory model that ingests historical influenza-like illness consultation rates and PM2.5 measurements yields lower-error short-term ILI forecasts than a standard recurrent neural network, and coupling these forecasts with WHO five-year outbreak thresholds enables threshold-based early warning of potential ILI surges.

source_questions.claim: Using weekly regional data from Taiwan, a long short-term memory model that ingests historical influenza-like illness consultation rates and PM2.5 measurements yields lower-error short-term ILI forecasts than a standard recurrent neural network, and coupling these forecasts with WHO five-year outbreak thresholds enables threshold-based early warning of potential ILI surges.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): Identify the statement that fully and accurately specifies the data scope, model inputs, comparative forecasting performance, and early-warning mechanism. [A] Using weekly regional data from Taiwan, an LSTM ingesting historical influenza-like illness consultation rates and PM2.5 achieved lower-error short-term ILI forecasts than a standard recurrent neural network, and coupling the forecasts with WHO five-year outbreak thresholds enabled threshold-based early warning of potential ILI surges. [B] Using daily national data from Taiwan, a standard recurrent neural network ingesting historical influenza-like illness consultation rates and ozone achieved lower-error long-term ILI forecasts than an LSTM, and coupling the forecasts with CDC ten-year thresholds enabled non-threshold early warning of influenza peaks. paper_title: Graph matching beyond perfectly-overlapping Erdős–Rényi random graphs
paper_claims: A partially-overlapping correlated Bernoulli networks model with overlap parameter s and correlation parameter ρ generalizes correlated Erdős–Rényi settings by allowing missing vertices while preserving identical marginal distributions and controlled cross-graph edge correlations.

Matching vertices by comparing 1‑Wasserstein distances between degree profile distributions and connecting each vertex to its top‑d candidates accommodates partial overlap without enforcing a bijection and produces candidate match sets.

Seeding with high‑degree vertices and refining via iterative linear assignment that maximizes common neighbors converts candidate sets into one‑to‑one alignments and provides per‑vertex convergence diagnostics.
Combining spectral community detection based on ratios of eigenvectors with within‑community degree‑profile matching and post‑processing enables graph matching under stochastic block models.

Degree‑profile‑based matching operates in polynomial time with distance computation O(n^3ρ + n^2) and subsequent steps O(n^2) and remains effective in sparse graphs where spectral methods typically fail.

source_questions.claim: A partially-overlapping correlated Bernoulli networks model with overlap parameter s and correlation parameter ρ generalizes correlated Erdős–Rényi settings by allowing missing vertices while preserving identical marginal distributions and controlled cross-graph edge correlations.

[B] It assumes full vertex alignment and changes marginal distributions while keeping edge correlations uncontrolled. [C] It replaces Bernoulli edges with Poisson edges and removes any cross-graph dependence. [D] It adds community structure but requires unequal marginals across graphs and no missing vertices.

[D] Requiring full vertex overlap, adding higher-order motifs, and fixing ρ = 0.

paper_title: Math-word embedding in math search and semantic extraction
paper_claims: Math-specific word embeddings trained on mathematical corpora capture semantic relationships among mathematical terms and expressions and can support tasks such as term similarity, analogy search, concept modeling, and query expansion.

Representing mathematical expressions as streams of tokens enables learning of similarities among mathematical objects but is inadequate for reliably linking identifiers to textual definiens due to ambiguity and context dependence.

The lack of precise, canonical semantic specifications for identifiers and functions in MathML/OpenMath and the absence of standardized datasets and benchmarks are major obstacles to effective machine learning for mathematical information retrieval and semantic extraction.

Embedding entire equations as single tokens facilitates comparison of equations, whereas recovering the semantics of individual identifiers requires structural grouping and canonicalization beyond current embedding techniques.
Word2vec-style embeddings trained on math texts can exhibit non-symmetric similarity behavior, limiting reliability for automated end-to-end retrieval scenarios.

[C] They are trained on mixed-domain corpora, capture only frequency statistics, and support keyword matching and deduplication. [D] They are hand-crafted symbolic rules that do not model semantics and cannot support analogy search or query expansion.

paper_title: PenQuest: a gamified attacker/defender meta model for cyber security assessment and education
paper_claims: A gamified attacker/defender meta model integrates service, information, and event layers with standardized knowledge bases (STIX, CAPEC, CVE/CWE) and NIST SP 800-53 controls to map concrete attack patterns and observable events to corresponding countermeasures for cyber risk assessment and education.

The rule system represents adversarial campaigns as a dynamic, asymmetric, non-cooperative, imperfect-information game with Bayesian elements, in which time-enabled actions affect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability status of interdependent services.

Parametric action definitions that include actor attributes, equipment effects, requirements, and success and detection chances, linked to attack patterns and event sequences, enable semantic correlation of monitoring data with attacker behavior and systematic selection of mitigating controls.

source_questions.claim: A gamified attacker/defender meta model integrates service, information, and event layers with standardized knowledge bases (STIX, CAPEC, CVE/CWE) and NIST SP 800-53 controls to map concrete attack patterns and observable events to corresponding countermeasures for cyber risk assessment and education.

paper_title: Quality attribute trade-offs in the embedded systems industry: an exploratory case study
paper_claims: Trade-offs between run-time and design-time qualities are common in embedded systems projects and are often implicit because teams lack adequate monitoring tools and practices.
Embedded systems practitioners manage quality attribute trade-offs primarily through lightweight, ad-hoc practices rather than formal processes or dedicated tooling to minimize overhead.
Continuous automated monitoring of relevant quality attributes enables explicit and prudent trade-off decisions and reduces the risk of accumulating technical debt in embedded systems.

Available tools in the embedded systems domain focus on individual quality attributes and provide little integrated support for managing trade-offs across attributes, creating demand for dedicated trade-off management tooling.

paper_title: Deep Insights into Convolutional Networks for Video Recognition
paper_claims: Cross-stream fusion in two-stream convolutional architectures induces units that integrate appearance and motion into coherent spatiotemporal features rather than independent cues.
The learned representation comprises both highly class-specific local detectors and generic units shared across classes, yielding distributed codes for action recognition.

With increasing network depth, feature selectivity becomes more abstract and increasingly invariant to nuisance factors such as motion speed, with motion units tuned to direction while tolerating a range of speeds and accelerations.

Early motion-stream layers implement spatiotemporal derivative-like filtering with temporally low-pass characteristics, whereas later layers capture higher-frequency temporal dynamics and exhibit stronger dependence on spatiotemporal regularization.

During supervised training, early-layer spatial filter structure remains stable while temporal selectivity progressively emerges, whereas higher-layer spatiotemporal filters undergo substantial structural changes.

[C] It preserves strict separation between streams so units process appearance and motion independently. [D] It eliminates spatial structure, leaving only global temporal signals.

paper_title: The dynamics of collaboration networks and the history of general relativity, 1925–1970
paper_claims: Integrating co-authorship, non-publishing collaborations, mentorship/influence ties, and institutional co-presence into a multilayer collaboration network reveals the emergence of a giant connected component in the general relativity community in the late 1950s–early 1960s, a transition that precedes 1960s astrophysical discoveries and is not reducible to postwar socio-economic changes.

paper_title: Simulating a rich ride-share mobility service using agent-based models
paper_claims: Coupling an agent-based demand model (MATSim) with an operational fleet simulator (IMSim) via co-simulation enables realistic, convergent assessment of autonomous ride-share mobility services by integrating personalized utility-based mode choice with endogenously computed waiting and detour times, multi-tier customer-segmented vehicle allocation, and refuel/recharge constraints to produce operator-, traveler-, and city-level performance measures.

[A] Realistic, convergent assessment by integrating personalized utility-based mode choice with endogenously computed waiting and detour times, multi-tier customer-segmented vehicle allocation, and refuel/recharge constraints to produce operator-, traveler-, and city-level performance measures [B] Fast exploratory simulations by assuming exogenous average waiting times, using single-tier vehicle dispatch without customer segmentation, and omitting refuel/recharge constraints to report only operator-level measures [C] Limited feasibility checks by coupling models sequentially, ignoring mode choice utilities, and focusing exclusively on traveler satisfaction without operator or city metrics [D] Scenario visualization by modeling fixed-route public transit with manual dispatching and post-processing traveler and city metrics without convergence

paper_title: A Multi-grained Log Auditing Scheme for Cloud Data Confidentiality
paper_claims: By aggregating data block accesses with Merkle Hash Trees and auditing an ordered AccessList of pseudonymous user identifiers across single blocks, multiple blocks, and entire files, a file-oriented cloud logging scheme achieves privacy-preserving confidentiality auditing and leakage investigation with reduced log volume and auditing overhead while maintaining forward-secure, append-only log integrity.

source_questions.claim: By aggregating data block accesses with Merkle Hash Trees and auditing an ordered AccessList of pseudonymous user identifiers across single blocks, multiple blocks, and entire files, a file-oriented cloud logging scheme achieves privacy-preserving confidentiality auditing and leakage investigation with reduced log volume and auditing overhead while maintaining forward-secure, append-only log integrity.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which description correctly states how a file-oriented cloud logging scheme achieves privacy-preserving confidentiality auditing and leakage investigation with reduced log volume and auditing overhead while maintaining forward-secure, append-only log integrity? [A] It aggregates data block accesses with Merkle Hash Trees and audits an ordered AccessList of pseudonymous user identifiers across single blocks, multiple blocks, and entire files. [B] It aggregates access events using Bloom filters and audits only per-user logs, focusing on availability checks with increased log volume and no forward security. [C] It uses Merkle Hash Trees but audits an unordered list only at the per-file level, achieving no leakage investigation and no reductions in volume or overhead. [D] It records each access individually with plaintext identities and relies on per-request signatures, offering confidentiality auditing without append-only integrity or overhead reductions.

paper_title: Correction of Scanning Electron Microscope Imaging Artifacts in a Novel Digital Image Correlation Framework
paper_claims: A unified integrated digital image correlation framework using a hierarchical composition of motion and imaging mappings simultaneously identifies and corrects SEM spatial distortion, drift distortion, and scan-line shifts while recovering mechanical displacement fields by modeling drift as a smooth time function projected onto scan times and separating it from mechanics with paired images per load step, calibrating time-invariant spatial distortion via rigid-body motions using spline or polynomial bases, representing random line shifts with localized error-function profiles, and performing time-integrated correlation with the average of all back-deformed images as the reference, thereby eliminating separate image-correction steps.

source_questions.claim: A unified integrated digital image correlation framework using a hierarchical composition of motion and imaging mappings simultaneously identifies and corrects SEM spatial distortion, drift distortion, and scan-line shifts while recovering mechanical displacement fields by modeling drift as a smooth time function projected onto scan times and separating it from mechanics with paired images per load step, calibrating time-invariant spatial distortion via rigid-body motions using spline or polynomial bases, representing random line shifts with localized error-function profiles, and performing time-integrated correlation with the average of all back-deformed images as the reference, thereby eliminating separate image-correction steps.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which description correctly specifies how a unified integrated digital image correlation framework using a hierarchical composition of motion and imaging mappings simultaneously identifies and corrects SEM spatial distortion, drift distortion, and scan-line shifts while recovering mechanical displacement fields and eliminating separate image-correction steps? [A] It models drift as a smooth time function projected onto scan times and separates it from mechanics using paired images per load step; calibrates time-invariant spatial distortion via rigid-body motions with spline or polynomial bases; represents random scan-line shifts using localized error-function profiles; and performs time-integrated correlation using the average of all back-deformed images as the reference. [B] It treats drift as piecewise-constant per frame and separates it from mechanics using single images; calibrates spatial distortion via nonrigid motion with Fourier bases; models line shifts as a global affine offset; and correlates against a single raw image, requiring pre-correction. [C] It sequentially corrects drift, spatial distortion, and line shifts using independent modules; estimates mechanical fields afterward; and references the first acquired image without time integration. [D] It relies on external calibration grids to remove all imaging distortions, assumes drift is negligible, estimates mechanics only, and averages raw images to define the reference.

source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which combination of modeling choices and reference selection characterizes a unified integrated DIC framework that, via a hierarchical composition of motion and imaging mappings, simultaneously corrects SEM spatial distortion, drift distortion, and scan-line shifts while recovering mechanical displacement fields and removing the need for separate image-correction steps? [A] Drift as white noise per line; spatial distortion from elastic deformations in Fourier bases; line shifts as global affine; reference is a single raw image. [C] Drift ignored; spatial distortion estimated per load step; line shifts corrected by median filtering; reference is the first deformed image. [D] Drift modeled by a step function per frame; spatial distortion corrected by external fiducials only; line shifts assumed periodic; reference is the median of raw images without back-deformation.

paper_title: A hybrid model for EEG-based gender recognition
paper_claims: Entropy-based features of resting-state EEG exhibit systematic gender differences across multiple scalp sites, providing discriminative information for gender recognition.

A hybrid classifier that encodes Random Forest leaf indices as sparse features for Logistic Regression yields superior EEG-based gender recognition compared with either Random Forest or Logistic Regression alone.
Fuzzy entropy features from EEG provide the strongest gender-discriminative power among the tested entropy measures and can match or exceed the performance of combined entropy feature sets.

Classification performance increases with the number of EEG electrodes, and the hybrid model shows greater robustness to variations in training data size and male–female class ratio than individual classifiers.

paper_title: XOR-ed visual secret sharing scheme with robust and meaningful shadows based on QR codes
paper_claims: Embedding XOR-ed random-grid visual secret sharing shadow bits into the padding codewords of QR codes preserves the codes’ decodability and error correction, producing meaningful shadows that enable (k,n)-threshold recovery of binary secret images with lossless reconstruction when all n shadows are combined and robustness to common image attacks.

paper_title: Temporally Coherent General Dynamic Scene Reconstruction
paper_claims: An unsupervised multi-view framework reconstructs temporally coherent 4D models of general dynamic scenes captured by static or moving wide-baseline cameras without prior scene or background knowledge by automatically initializing coarse object segmentation and geometry from sparse 3D features, propagating sparse-to-dense temporal correspondences to detect and track dynamic regions, and jointly refining per-view depth and multi-view segmentation under a geodesic star-convexity shape constraint to obtain surfaces with consistent labels and correspondence across time.

source_questions.claim: An unsupervised multi-view framework reconstructs temporally coherent 4D models of general dynamic scenes captured by static or moving wide-baseline cameras without prior scene or background knowledge by automatically initializing coarse object segmentation and geometry from sparse 3D features, propagating sparse-to-dense temporal correspondences to detect and track dynamic regions, and jointly refining per-view depth and multi-view segmentation under a geodesic star-convexity shape constraint to obtain surfaces with consistent labels and correspondence across time.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which statement accurately and completely describes a framework that reconstructs temporally coherent 4D models of general dynamic scenes? [B] A supervised multi-view framework relies on pre-labeled backgrounds, assumes narrow-baseline static cameras, initializes from dense optical flow, and performs independent per-view depth optimization without shape constraints, producing per-frame surfaces without temporal correspondences. [C] An unsupervised single-view framework requires a prior scene model, tracks motion by keyframe selection only, and refines segmentation alone without imposing any shape constraint, yielding temporally incoherent reconstructions. [D] A semi-supervised multi-view pipeline reconstructs only static scenes by aggregating per-frame meshes from stereo pairs and post-hoc label smoothing, assuming background subtraction is given.

paper_title: Refractive Two-View Reconstruction for Underwater 3D Vision
paper_claims: Refraction at a flat interface renders the single-viewpoint approximation invalid and induces depth-dependent geometric bias in underwater imaging when using pinhole-plus-distortion models.

In the case of a pinhole camera observing through a known thin, flat refractive interface, the two-view geometry admits a refractive fundamental matrix that depends on the interface’s position and orientation and the relative camera motion.

Using the refractive fundamental constraint together with refractive reprojection constraints in a two-step estimation improves outlier rejection and yields more accurate and robust underwater relative pose and quasi-dense 3D reconstruction than standard two-view Structure-from-Motion.

paper_title: Disentangling Geometry and Appearance with Regularised Geometry-Aware Generative Adversarial Networks
paper_claims: Conditioning a generative adversarial network on a statistical shape model and enforcing the prior by differentiably warping outputs to a canonical frame disentangles shape from appearance, provides explicit control over object geometry while maintaining realistic texture, constrains the search space to enable effective training on small datasets, and serves as a modular augmentation applicable to diverse GAN architectures.

Training the discriminator with (α,β) regularization to accept slight shape perturbations and reject large ones under the warping transform mitigates label and covariate shift and produces reliable image–shape alignment scores that enable detection of facial landmark tracking or detection errors.

source_questions.claim: Conditioning a generative adversarial network on a statistical shape model and enforcing the prior by differentiably warping outputs to a canonical frame disentangles shape from appearance, provides explicit control over object geometry while maintaining realistic texture, constrains the search space to enable effective training on small datasets, and serves as a modular augmentation applicable to diverse GAN architectures.

[B] It couples shape and appearance, reduces texture realism, increases the search space requiring large datasets, and is tailored to a single GAN architecture. [C] It only improves texture realism without affecting geometry control, does not constrain the search space, and cannot be used across different GAN architectures. [D] It eliminates the need for a shape prior or warping, yet still provides geometry control and generalizes only with extensive data.

[C] Disentanglement of shape from appearance, explicit control over object geometry with realistic textures, a constrained search space that enables effective training on small datasets, and modular compatibility with diverse GAN architectures. [D] Exclusive enhancement of appearance modeling at the expense of geometry and portability across architectures.

source_questions.question: Assertion (A): Conditioning a GAN on a statistical shape model and enforcing the prior by differentiably warping outputs to a canonical frame disentangles shape from appearance, provides explicit control over object geometry while maintaining realistic texture, constrains the search space to enable effective training on small datasets, and serves as a modular augmentation applicable to diverse GAN architectures. Reason (R): An explicit shape prior restricts geometric variability, and architecture-agnostic differentiable warping aligns outputs in a common coordinate system, which separates geometry from texture, reduces the effective hypothesis space, and allows the mechanism to be inserted into different GANs.
source_questions.answer: A is True, R is True, and R explains A

source_questions.claim: Conditioning a generative adversarial network on a statistical shape model and enforcing the prior by differentiably warping outputs to a canonical frame disentangles shape from appearance, provides explicit control over object geometry while maintaining realistic texture, constrains the search space to enable effective training on small datasets, and serves as a modular augmentation applicable to diverse GAN architectures.
source_questions.question: Assertion (A): Conditioning a GAN on a statistical shape model and enforcing the prior by differentiably warping outputs to a canonical frame disentangles shape from appearance, provides explicit control over object geometry while maintaining realistic texture, constrains the search space to enable effective training on small datasets, and serves as a modular augmentation applicable to diverse GAN architectures. Reason (R): Differentiable warping to a canonical frame primarily forces joint learning of shape and appearance, increases the search space, and prevents reuse across architectures.
source_questions.answer: A is True, R is False

source_questions.claim: Training the discriminator with (α,β) regularization to accept slight shape perturbations and reject large ones under the warping transform mitigates label and covariate shift and produces reliable image–shape alignment scores that enable detection of facial landmark tracking or detection errors.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which statement accurately characterizes training a discriminator with (α,β) regularization to accept slight shape perturbations and reject large ones under the warping transform? [A] It mitigates label and covariate shift and yields reliable image–shape alignment scores that enable detection of facial landmark tracking or detection errors. [B] It amplifies label and covariate shift and yields unstable alignment scores that hinder detection of facial landmark tracking or detection errors. [C] It mitigates only covariate shift but not label shift, and produces scores unsuitable for detecting facial landmark tracking or detection errors. [D] It ignores the warping transform and relies on hand-crafted features, providing alignment scores for segmentation errors rather than facial landmark issues.

source_questions.claim: Training the discriminator with (α,β) regularization to accept slight shape perturbations and reject large ones under the warping transform mitigates label and covariate shift and produces reliable image–shape alignment scores that enable detection of facial landmark tracking or detection errors.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): Under a warping transform, training a discriminator with (α,β) regularization has which combined objective and outcome? [A] Reject slight perturbations and accept large ones; mitigates shifts; enables detection of landmark errors using reliable alignment scores. [B] Accept slight perturbations and reject large ones; does not address label or covariate shift; yields unreliable scores unrelated to landmark error detection. [C] Accept slight perturbations and reject large ones; mitigates label and covariate shift; produces reliable image–shape alignment scores that enable detection of facial landmark tracking or detection errors. [D] Accept and reject perturbations indiscriminately; addresses only label shift; detects segmentation errors but not facial landmark errors.

paper_title: Robust optimization and uncertainty quantification in the nonlinear mechanics of an elevator brake system
paper_claims: In a mechanical equilibrium model of a friction elevator safety gear, the braking force is an explicit function of geometry, friction coefficients and loads, and in realistic operating ranges it is weakly sensitive to cam angle while varying approximately affinely with the spring reaction force, making spring force uncertainty the dominant contributor to braking force variability.

A robust optimization that maximizes a convex combination of worst-case, best-case, mean and inverse standard deviation of braking force under a probabilistic minimum-force constraint produces geometric designs that are less sensitive to parameter uncertainties than those obtained by deterministic optimization.

source_questions.claim: In a mechanical equilibrium model of a friction elevator safety gear, the braking force is an explicit function of geometry, friction coefficients and loads, and in realistic operating ranges it is weakly sensitive to cam angle while varying approximately affinely with the spring reaction force, making spring force uncertainty the dominant contributor to braking force variability.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): In a mechanical equilibrium model of a friction elevator safety gear, which statement correctly characterizes how braking force depends on system parameters and varies across realistic operating ranges? [A] The braking force is an explicit function of geometry, friction coefficients, and loads; in realistic ranges it is weakly sensitive to cam angle, varies approximately affinely with the spring reaction force, and spring force uncertainty dominates braking force variability. [B] The braking force is an implicit function determined numerically; in realistic ranges it is highly sensitive to cam angle, varies nonlinearly with the spring reaction force, and cam angle uncertainty dominates variability. [C] The braking force depends only on loads; it is insensitive to cam angle and independent of the spring reaction force, so friction coefficient uncertainty dominates variability. [D] The braking force is an explicit function of geometry and friction coefficients only; in realistic ranges it is strongly sensitive to cam angle and varies quadratically with the spring reaction force, so geometric tolerances dominate variability.

source_questions.claim: In a mechanical equilibrium model of a friction elevator safety gear, the braking force is an explicit function of geometry, friction coefficients and loads, and in realistic operating ranges it is weakly sensitive to cam angle while varying approximately affinely with the spring reaction force, making spring force uncertainty the dominant contributor to braking force variability.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): For a mechanical equilibrium representation of a friction elevator safety gear, which combination of dependencies and sensitivities is consistent with the model’s conclusions across realistic operating ranges? [A] Explicit dependence on geometry, friction coefficients, and loads; weak sensitivity to cam angle; approximately affine variation with spring reaction force; and dominance of spring force uncertainty in braking force variability. [B] Explicit dependence on geometry and loads only; strong sensitivity to cam angle; approximately affine variation with spring reaction force; and dominance of cam angle uncertainty in variability. [C] Implicit dependence on geometry, friction coefficients, and loads; weak sensitivity to cam angle; nonlinear dependence on spring reaction force; and dominance of friction coefficient uncertainty in variability. [D] Explicit dependence on geometry, friction coefficients, and loads; strong sensitivity to cam angle; nonlinear dependence on spring reaction force; and dominance of geometric uncertainty in variability.

source_questions.claim: In a mechanical equilibrium model of a friction elevator safety gear, the braking force is an explicit function of geometry, friction coefficients and loads, and in realistic operating ranges it is weakly sensitive to cam angle while varying approximately affinely with the spring reaction force, making spring force uncertainty the dominant contributor to braking force variability.
source_questions.question: Assertion (A): In a mechanical equilibrium model of a friction elevator safety gear, the braking force is an explicit function of geometry, friction coefficients, and loads and, over realistic operating ranges, is weakly sensitive to cam angle while varying approximately affinely with the spring reaction force, making spring force uncertainty the dominant contributor to braking force variability. Reason (R): Because the force-balance equations yield a closed-form dependence on geometry, friction, and loads, and within realistic ranges the cam angle contributes only minor (higher-order) effects whereas the spring reaction enters nearly linearly, so uncertainty in the spring force propagates most strongly to the braking force.
source_questions.answer: A is True, R is True, and R explains A

source_questions.claim: In a mechanical equilibrium model of a friction elevator safety gear, the braking force is an explicit function of geometry, friction coefficients and loads, and in realistic operating ranges it is weakly sensitive to cam angle while varying approximately affinely with the spring reaction force, making spring force uncertainty the dominant contributor to braking force variability.
source_questions.question: Assertion (A): In a mechanical equilibrium model of a friction elevator safety gear, the braking force depends explicitly on geometry, friction coefficients, and loads, is weakly sensitive to cam angle in realistic operating ranges, varies approximately affinely with the spring reaction force, and has variability dominated by spring force uncertainty. Reason (R): Because the braking force in this model is insensitive to geometry and friction and is determined mainly by cam angle variations.
source_questions.answer: A is True, R is False

[D] Their sensitivity cannot be determined from this information.

paper_title: Disk Cluster Allocation Behavior in Windows and NTFS
paper_claims: NTFS on Windows 7 and Windows 10 generally follows a best-fit free-space allocation strategy for block-written files but exhibits systematic deviations where allocations are taken from large areas despite smaller better-fitting holes and fragments are placed leaving small residual gaps.

On fragmented volumes, block-written file fragments decrease in size both within each file and across successive files, whereas stream-written files begin with very small fragments and progressively increase fragment sizes as writing proceeds.

source_questions.claim: NTFS on Windows 7 and Windows 10 generally follows a best-fit free-space allocation strategy for block-written files but exhibits systematic deviations where allocations are taken from large areas despite smaller better-fitting holes and fragments are placed leaving small residual gaps.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): On Windows 7 and Windows 10, NTFS free-space allocation for block-written files generally follows which pattern, including how it deviates in practice? [A] A generally best-fit allocation with systematic deviations: allocations are sometimes taken from large areas despite smaller better-fitting holes, and fragments are placed leaving small residual gaps. [B] A strict best-fit allocation with no deviations: the smallest fitting hole is always chosen and residual gaps are never left. [C] A primarily first-fit allocation with deviations that favor smaller holes over larger areas, eliminating small gaps. [D] A worst-fit allocation that consistently takes from the largest areas specifically to avoid any residual gaps.

[B] It always selects the smallest available hole and coalesces fragments to avoid residual gaps. [C] It randomly selects holes with no relation to size and fully eliminates gaps via compaction. [D] It predominantly uses first-fit and avoids allocating from large areas when smaller holes exist.

Reason (R): Allocations sometimes come from large free areas even when smaller better-fitting holes exist, and fragment placement can leave small residual gaps.

paper_title: A new design for cubic spatially processed wideband arrays
paper_claims: A cubic volumetric array with a single real multiplier per element can steer a fully spatial beam throughout full azimuth and elevation hemispheres while maintaining frequency-invariant beampatterns over a wide fractional bandwidth.

Real-valued element weights are determined by representing the array response as a sum of L symmetric steering terms centered at selected focal points in a bounded three-dimensional frequency space and solving a reduced L-by-L linear system, where even L and symmetric point placement ensure real weights and unit gain in the desired direction.

The design requires solving a linear system of dimension L that can be chosen much smaller than the total number of array elements, thereby substantially reducing computational complexity relative to direct weight computation.

With inter-element spacings not exceeding half the minimum wavelength, the transformed frequency variables are confined to a cube (−0.5, 0.5) in each dimension, supporting frequency-invariant array responses within the operational band.
Increasing the number of symmetric focal points narrows the main lobe and suppresses sidelobes without compromising steering capability or frequency invariance.

source_questions.claim: A cubic volumetric array with a single real multiplier per element can steer a fully spatial beam throughout full azimuth and elevation hemispheres while maintaining frequency-invariant beampatterns over a wide fractional bandwidth.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which statement correctly describes an array that can steer a fully spatial beam across the entire azimuth and elevation hemispheres while maintaining frequency-invariant beampatterns over a wide fractional bandwidth? [A] A cubic volumetric array with a single real multiplier per element. [B] A planar array with per-element complex multipliers that steers only in azimuth and is narrowband. [C] A linear array with a single real multiplier per element that provides azimuth-only steering and frequency-dependent beampatterns. [D] A cubic volumetric array using per-element time delays that produces beampatterns varying with frequency over a narrow bandwidth.

source_questions.claim: A cubic volumetric array with a single real multiplier per element can steer a fully spatial beam throughout full azimuth and elevation hemispheres while maintaining frequency-invariant beampatterns over a wide fractional bandwidth.

[A] A spherical conformal array with two real multipliers per element. [B] A cubic volumetric array with per-element complex phase shifters and narrow fractional bandwidth. [C] A cubic volumetric array employing a single real multiplier per element. [D] A rectangular planar array with a single real multiplier per element and azimuth-only steering.

Reason (R): Its three-dimensional aperture supports steering in both azimuth and elevation, and per-element weighting that does not vary with frequency helps preserve the beampattern shape across a wide fractional bandwidth.
source_questions.answer: A is True, R is True, and R explains A

paper_title: Local dimension reduction of summary statistics for likelihood-free inference
paper_claims: A localization strategy for projection-based dimension reduction in likelihood-free inference, where transformations are fitted in a neighborhood of the observed data and localization and transformation parameters are optimized using validation datasets, yields informative low-dimensional summaries that improve posterior estimation accuracy and stability and mitigate failure of transformed summaries outside the local region across diverse models.

source_questions.claim: A localization strategy for projection-based dimension reduction in likelihood-free inference, where transformations are fitted in a neighborhood of the observed data and localization and transformation parameters are optimized using validation datasets, yields informative low-dimensional summaries that improve posterior estimation accuracy and stability and mitigate failure of transformed summaries outside the local region across diverse models.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): In likelihood-free inference with projection-based dimension reduction, which statement correctly describes a localization strategy that fits transformations in a neighborhood of the observed data and optimizes both localization and transformation parameters using validation datasets, and its outcomes across diverse models? [A] It yields informative low-dimensional summaries that improve posterior estimation accuracy and stability and mitigate failure of transformed summaries outside the local region. [B] It yields low-dimensional summaries but does not improve posterior accuracy or stability and has no effect on failures outside the local region. [C] It relies on globally fitted transformations without validation-based optimization, providing general improvements regardless of model diversity. [D] It fits transformations far from the observed data and tunes only transformation parameters using training data, leading to less informative summaries and model-specific instability.

source_questions.claim: A localization strategy for projection-based dimension reduction in likelihood-free inference, where transformations are fitted in a neighborhood of the observed data and localization and transformation parameters are optimized using validation datasets, yields informative low-dimensional summaries that improve posterior estimation accuracy and stability and mitigate failure of transformed summaries outside the local region across diverse models.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): Which configuration and outcome best aligns with a localization approach for projection-based dimension reduction in likelihood-free inference across diverse models? [A] Fitting transformations in the observed-data neighborhood and optimizing localization and transformation parameters with validation datasets, producing informative low-dimensional summaries that enhance posterior accuracy and stability while reducing failure of transformed summaries outside the local region. [B] Fitting transformations globally and fixing parameters a priori, producing summaries that marginally compress data but often degrade posterior accuracy and exacerbate out-of-region failures. [C] Fitting transformations near observed data but optimizing only transformation parameters on training data, yielding stable posteriors yet not informative summaries and no mitigation outside the local region. [D] Using no localization but cross-validated feature selection, which ensures improvements only within a single model and may increase instability.

source_questions.claim: A localization strategy for projection-based dimension reduction in likelihood-free inference, where transformations are fitted in a neighborhood of the observed data and localization and transformation parameters are optimized using validation datasets, yields informative low-dimensional summaries that improve posterior estimation accuracy and stability and mitigate failure of transformed summaries outside the local region across diverse models.

paper_title: GPU acceleration of splitting schemes applied to differential matrix equations
paper_claims: For differential Lyapunov equations, approximating the integral representation with high-order quadrature using matrix exponential actions delivers superior accuracy and efficiency compared to Strang splitting.

For differential Riccati equations, splitting into the minimal number of subproblems—grouping the linear and constant terms and separating the nonlinear term—generally minimizes splitting error, whereas additionally splitting the constant term becomes advantageous when that term is sufficiently small.

In generalized equations with multiplicative terms of the form S P S^T, treating that subproblem by the midpoint rule within Strang splitting preserves second-order convergence and admits efficient low-rank updates through block augmentation and column compression.

source_questions.claim: For differential Lyapunov equations, approximating the integral representation with high-order quadrature using matrix exponential actions delivers superior accuracy and efficiency compared to Strang splitting.
source_questions.question: For _________(differential Lyapunov equations/algebraic Riccati equations), approximating the integral representation with high-order quadrature using matrix exponential actions delivers superior accuracy and efficiency compared to Strang splitting.

source_questions.claim: For differential Lyapunov equations, approximating the integral representation with high-order quadrature using matrix exponential actions delivers superior accuracy and efficiency compared to Strang splitting.
source_questions.question: Approximating the integral representation with high-order quadrature using matrix exponential actions for differential Lyapunov equations is _________(superior/inferior) in accuracy and efficiency relative to Strang splitting.

source_questions.claim: For differential Lyapunov equations, approximating the integral representation with high-order quadrature using matrix exponential actions delivers superior accuracy and efficiency compared to Strang splitting.

paper_claims: The maximum a posteriori estimation for this model admits a strictly convex reformulation in the precision parameters that is solvable to global optimality using a three-block ADMM algorithm with convergence guaranteed for any positive penalty parameter.

paper_title: Enabling connectivity for tactical networks in mountainous areas by aerial relays
paper_claims: Integrating high-resolution 3D terrain, slope-dependent mobility traces, antenna radiation patterns, and irregular-terrain propagation modeling indicates that in mountainous tactical deployments a single tethered low-altitude balloon relay equipped with a tilted helical antenna can maintain line-of-sight star connectivity among platoon cluster heads, with reliable dissemination achieved via time-shared unicast using amplify/decode-and-forward relaying and go-back-N retransmissions.

paper_title: Person Re-identification in Identity Regression Space
paper_claims: Formulating person re-identification as regression into an identity regression space where each training identity is assigned an orthogonal unit target vector yields an embedding that collapses intra-class variance and maximizes inter-class separateness.

The regression-based re-identification model supports online incremental learning by updating the projection with Sherman–Morrison–Woodbury identities using only newly labelled samples, eliminating retraining and past-data storage.

Active learning that jointly exploits exploration of appearance diversity and unexplained cross-view matching discrepancy prioritizes informative samples for annotation, improving label efficiency during incremental updates.

Encoding class targets as orthogonal one-hot vectors strengthens global between-class discrimination while retaining extreme intra-class compactness compared to null-space Fisher and FDA-style embeddings.

paper_title: A diachronic perspective on peer disagreement in veritistic social epistemology
paper_claims: In a Bayesian, veritistic evaluation of peer disagreement that updates both credences and inter-agent trust, the conciliatory response of lowering confidence and trust upon unexpected disagreement increases expected truth-approximation only when agents continue to recheck the target proposition over time, whereas without such ongoing inquiry it confers no veritistic advantage over steadfastness.

source_questions.claim: In a Bayesian, veritistic evaluation of peer disagreement that updates both credences and inter-agent trust, the conciliatory response of lowering confidence and trust upon unexpected disagreement increases expected truth-approximation only when agents continue to recheck the target proposition over time, whereas without such ongoing inquiry it confers no veritistic advantage over steadfastness.

[A] It increases expected truth-approximation only if agents continue to recheck the target proposition over time; without ongoing inquiry it offers no advantage over steadfastness. [B] It increases expected truth-approximation irrespective of whether agents recheck, and thus strictly dominates steadfastness. [C] It never increases expected truth-approximation, even with continued rechecking over time. [D] It increases expected truth-approximation only when agents halt further inquiry.

[C] Only with continued rechecking of the target proposition over time does it improve expected truth-approximation; without such inquiry it offers no veritistic advantage over steadfastness. [D] Only when trust is not updated does it improve expected truth-approximation, regardless of rechecking.

paper_title: Face Mask Extraction in Video Sequence
paper_claims: A ConvLSTM-augmented fully convolutional network that processes video sequences learns temporal dependencies alongside spatial features to perform end-to-end dense segmentation of facial components in video.

A segmentation loss derived from the intersection-over-union objective that assigns separate per-class weights to positive and negative samples directly optimizes mean IoU and mitigates class imbalance more effectively than cross-entropy and naive IoU losses.
A coarse-to-fine pipeline that combines a primary whole-face model with zoomed-in eye and mouth models yields more accurate component masks than a single-scale model alone.

[B] It processes single images, ignores temporal information, and performs two-stage sparse keypoint detection of generic objects. [C] It processes video sequences but learns only spatial features and outputs image-level emotion classification. [D] It processes optical flow exclusively and produces bounding-box tracking of faces.

paper_title: Reducing false wake-up in contention-based wake-up control of wireless LANs
paper_claims: In wake-up radio assisted contention-based transmitter wake-up for WLANs, the WLAN module’s wake-up latency induces multiple false activations that dominate energy overhead relative to collisions, and jointly applying backoff counter resetting to emulate freezing during wake-up, early sleep upon inferred false activation, and contention window tuning reduces false-wake probability and duration while improving the throughput–energy tradeoff.

False wake-up probability increases with the WLAN module wake-up latency, and for sufficiently large contention windows the ratio of false wake-ups to collisions approaches the wake-up period expressed in slots.

Selecting the contention window by maximizing the product of spectral energy efficiency and channel efficiency, using wake-up radio observations of idle intervals to inform contention levels, yields higher overall efficiency than configurations based solely on throughput or collision energy.

source_questions.claim: In wake-up radio assisted contention-based transmitter wake-up for WLANs, the WLAN module’s wake-up latency induces multiple false activations that dominate energy overhead relative to collisions, and jointly applying backoff counter resetting to emulate freezing during wake-up, early sleep upon inferred false activation, and contention window tuning reduces false-wake probability and duration while improving the throughput–energy tradeoff.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): In wake-up radio-assisted contention-based transmitter wake-up for WLANs, which statement correctly describes the dominant source of energy overhead and the effect of a specific joint mitigation? [A] The WLAN module’s wake-up latency induces multiple false activations that dominate energy overhead relative to collisions, and jointly applying backoff counter resetting to emulate freezing during wake-up, early sleep upon inferred false activation, and contention window tuning reduces false-wake probability and duration while improving the throughput–energy tradeoff. [B] Wake-up latency has negligible impact on activation patterns, collisions dominate energy overhead, and enabling RTS/CTS with larger payloads eliminates false wakes but degrades the throughput–energy tradeoff. [C] Wake-up latency decreases the likelihood of false activations, collisions chiefly determine energy overhead, and applying only contention window tuning lowers false-wake duration but worsens the throughput–energy tradeoff. [D] Wake-up latency causes multiple false activations, yet energy overhead is dominated by collisions, and using transmit power control and higher carrier sensing thresholds reduces collisions but degrades the throughput–energy tradeoff.

source_questions.claim: In wake-up radio assisted contention-based transmitter wake-up for WLANs, the WLAN module’s wake-up latency induces multiple false activations that dominate energy overhead relative to collisions, and jointly applying backoff counter resetting to emulate freezing during wake-up, early sleep upon inferred false activation, and contention window tuning reduces false-wake probability and duration while improving the throughput–energy tradeoff.

[C] Disabling backoff, extending beacon intervals, and fixing small contention windows. [D] Increasing carrier sense thresholds, aggressive retransmissions, and delayed sleeping.

paper_title: Probabilistic Approach Versus Machine Learning for One-Shot Quad-Tree Prediction in an Intra HEVC Encoder
paper_claims: For HEVC intra coding, per-depth thresholds on CU luminance variance derived from a learning frame and modeled as a linear function of the mean and standard deviation of the variance distribution enable one-shot prediction of CTU quad-tree partitioning largely independent of quantization parameter.

Lightweight decision-tree classifiers using local variance-based features of CUs, sub-CUs, neighboring CUs, and QP accurately predict per-depth split/merge decisions, enabling one-shot quad-tree partitioning without exhaustive RDO.

Constraining rate–distortion optimization to the interval between an initial variance-threshold-based depth map and a refined depth map drastically reduces quad-tree search complexity while preserving compression performance within a controlled trade-off.

[B] Global thresholds on CU chroma variance, derived from each test frame and modeled as a nonlinear function of the variance distribution's median and range. [C] Per-CTU thresholds on CU luminance variance, learned without a learning frame and fixed across all depths, modeled as constants. [D] Per-depth thresholds on CU edge density, derived from a learning frame and modeled as a quadratic function of the mean and standard deviation, tuned for each quantization parameter.

[D] For HEVC intra coding, per-depth CU luminance variance thresholds from a learning frame modeled as a quadratic function of the mean and standard deviation, with decisions strictly tied to a fixed quantization parameter.

paper_title: QEX: target-specific druglikeness filter enhances ligand-based virtual screening
paper_claims: A target-specific druglikeness index constructed by modeling the distributions of eight physicochemical properties from known actives and aggregating them via weighted desirability functions provides superior enrichment of actives for individual targets compared with a global druglikeness metric trained on approved drugs.

By relying solely on active compounds and capturing binding-pocket physicochemical preferences, a target-specific druglikeness index enables effective screening when inactive data are unavailable and complements oral-drug-focused indices that emphasize ADMET-related constraints.

paper_title: An adaptive LTE listen-before-talk scheme towards a fair coexistence with Wi-Fi in unlicensed spectrum
paper_claims: Fixed LTE Licensed-Assisted Access channel occupancy durations that are much longer than single-packet Wi‑Fi transmissions, particularly when Wi‑Fi does not aggregate frames, create imbalanced channel occupancy that can yield unfair coexistence in co-channel deployments.

An adaptive LTE‑U listen‑before‑talk mechanism that performs clear channel assessment before each burst and then uses a tunable transmission opportunity followed by a tunable muting period, with subframe‑aligned reservation signaling, enables fair, technology‑agnostic coexistence with Wi‑Fi and other unlicensed systems by balancing access opportunities according to network density and traffic requirements while preserving time‑critical LTE signaling on licensed spectrum.

Fair coexistence in unlicensed bands is more appropriately defined as equal sharing of wireless resources among co‑located networks rather than among different radio technologies, with throughput and channel occupancy serving as practical fairness indicators.

source_questions.claim: Fixed LTE Licensed-Assisted Access channel occupancy durations that are much longer than single-packet Wi‑Fi transmissions, particularly when Wi‑Fi does not aggregate frames, create imbalanced channel occupancy that can yield unfair coexistence in co-channel deployments.
source_questions.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): In co-channel deployments, which scenario matches the statement that fixed LTE Licensed-Assisted Access (LAA) channel occupancy durations much longer than single-packet Wi‑Fi transmissions, particularly when Wi‑Fi does not aggregate frames, create imbalanced channel occupancy that can yield unfair coexistence? [A] LAA uses fixed, long channel occupancy bursts relative to single-packet, non-aggregated Wi‑Fi, leading to disproportionate airtime and potential unfair coexistence. [B] LAA uses very short channel occupancy bursts comparable to Wi‑Fi aggregated transmissions, producing balanced airtime and fair coexistence. [C] LAA and Wi‑Fi operate on different channels, eliminating coexistence concerns regardless of occupancy durations. [D] Wi‑Fi aggregates frames while LAA varies its occupancy adaptively, ensuring fairness independent of relative burst lengths.

paper_title: Providing Reliable Communications over Static-node-assisted Vehicular Networks Using Distance-vector Routing
paper_claims: RDV is a table-driven distance-vector routing protocol for static-node-assisted vehicular delay-tolerant networks that, using statistic-based vehicle selection to estimate local movement probabilities and adaptive packet duplication to satisfy a preconfigured expected end-to-end delivery ratio, computes shortest paths with a traffic-aware metric through lightweight neighbor exchanges without roadmaps, thereby enabling reliable communication under sparse and unreliable static-node deployment with low control overhead.

source_questions.claim: RDV is a table-driven distance-vector routing protocol for static-node-assisted vehicular delay-tolerant networks that, using statistic-based vehicle selection to estimate local movement probabilities and adaptive packet duplication to satisfy a preconfigured expected end-to-end delivery ratio, computes shortest paths with a traffic-aware metric through lightweight neighbor exchanges without roadmaps, thereby enabling reliable communication under sparse and unreliable static-node deployment with low control overhead.

[A] A table-driven distance-vector protocol for static-node-assisted vehicular delay-tolerant networks that estimates local movement probabilities via statistic-based vehicle selection, adaptively duplicates packets to meet a preconfigured expected end-to-end delivery ratio, computes shortest paths using a traffic-aware metric through lightweight neighbor exchanges without roadmaps, and thereby provides reliable communication under sparse and unreliable static-node deployment with low control overhead. [B] A reactive link-state protocol for general mobile ad hoc networks that relies on preloaded roadmaps and global traffic maps, avoids packet duplication to minimize bandwidth, and emphasizes maximum-throughput routes even when static nodes are dense, with high control overhead. [C] A geographic greedy protocol for vehicular networks without static nodes, which selects next hops solely by instantaneous GPS positions, floods packets to maximize delivery, computes minimal-hop paths without traffic awareness, and requires detailed roadmaps. [D] A table-driven distance-vector protocol for static-node-assisted vehicular DTNs that uses fixed-k replication without movement estimation, computes shortest paths by hop count with periodic full-table exchanges and city roadmaps, achieving reliability only when static nodes are dense with moderate overhead.

paper_title: Estimation of and correction for finite motion sampling errors in small animal PET rigid motion correction
paper_claims: In rigid motion–corrected small animal PET, a voxel-wise residual motion deconvolution that derives spatially variant 3D kernels from measured motion by modeling pose uncertainty within sampling intervals and applies them via Richardson–Lucy deconvolution reduces the finite-sampling–induced, motion-speed–dependent resolution loss and associated regional quantification biases to a level comparable to motion correction using pose interpolation.

paper_title: Discovering the Hidden Community Structure of Public Transportation Networks
paper_claims: A transfer network that represents atomic passenger groups as maximal cliques linked by directed transfer edges, together with a community network that connects passenger pairs using a connection-strength metric that rewards shared transfers across different vehicle trips and penalizes repeated co-presence within the same trip, captures similarity in rider travel behavior beyond contact duration alone.

In an activity-based simulation of the Twin Cities public transit system, frequent multi-trip travel corridors primarily link outlying suburbs to the city center during peak hours and epidemic spreading risk concentrates on routes that cross or connect to the city center.

source_questions.claim: A transfer network that represents atomic passenger groups as maximal cliques linked by directed transfer edges, together with a community network that connects passenger pairs using a connection-strength metric that rewards shared transfers across different vehicle trips and penalizes repeated co-presence within the same trip, captures similarity in rider travel behavior beyond contact duration alone.

[D] The transfer network uses random subgraphs with directed edges, the community network links any pair sharing a trip without weighting, and the result captures only contact duration.

paper_title: In vivo estimation of passive biomechanical properties of human myocardium
paper_claims: Maintaining fixed ratios among the anisotropic parameters (af:as:afs and bf:bs:bfs) and estimating only four scalars (a, b, Ka, Kb) preserves the experimentally observed shear stiffness ordering and suffices to calibrate the model from limited data.

Identified passive myocardial parameters depend more strongly on the assumed left-ventricular end-diastolic pressure than on ventricular geometry or fibre helix angle, with the stiffness nonlinearity parameter b being most sensitive.
