paper_title: Approximation of quantum control correction scheme using deep neural networks
paper_claims: Bidirectional long short-term memory networks approximate the functional mapping from normal control pulses for an ideal two-qubit system to de-noising pulses that compensate coherent drift, enabling generation of effective controls without explicit system modelling.

The learnability and accuracy of the NCP-to-DCP mapping depend on the drift structure, being easier when the drift Hamiltonian resembles the base spin-chain interaction and harder for asymmetric single-qubit drifts orthogonal to the available controls.

The learned mapping exhibits continuity and approximate symmetry with respect to local perturbations of the input pulses, as small changes in control amplitudes at nearby times or along different control axes induce statistically similar variations in the corrected pulses over mid evolution windows.
For weak Markovian incoherent noise modeled by Lindblad operators, learning-based correction of control pulses does not provide a meaningful performance gain over uncorrected pulses.

Optimizing for robustness by training on Gaussian-amplitude–perturbed copies of the generated pulses fails to produce controls with improved fluctuation resilience, indicating limitations of gradient-based learning for this robustness objective.

source_questions.question: By combining a 3D local intensity clustering-based level set that jointly estimates MRI bias fields with normal-vector intensity line profiling and variance-minimizing, thickness-constrained optimization, automatic delineation of trabecular and cortical bone of the femur and tibia in _________(proton density weighted/T1-weighted) knee MRI is achieved without training data and with robustness to intensity inhomogeneity and weak edges near ligaments.

source_questions.claim: Combining a 3D local intensity clustering-based level set that jointly estimates MRI bias fields with normal-vector intensity line profiling and variance-minimizing, thickness-constrained optimization enables automatic delineation of trabecular and cortical bone of the femur and tibia in proton density weighted knee MRI without training data, robust to intensity inhomogeneity and weak edges near ligaments.
source_questions.question: Assertion (A): Combining a bias-field–estimating 3D local intensity clustering level set with normal-vector intensity line profiling and variance-minimizing, thickness-constrained optimization enables automatic delineation of trabecular and cortical bone of the femur and tibia in proton density weighted knee MRI without training data and is robust to intensity inhomogeneity and weak edges near ligaments. Reason (R): This is because the combination integrates joint MRI bias field estimation with boundary profiling along normals and thickness-constrained variance minimization.
source_questions.answer: A is True, R is True, and R explains A

source_questions.claim: Combining a 3D local intensity clustering-based level set that jointly estimates MRI bias fields with normal-vector intensity line profiling and variance-minimizing, thickness-constrained optimization enables automatic delineation of trabecular and cortical bone of the femur and tibia in proton density weighted knee MRI without training data, robust to intensity inhomogeneity and weak edges near ligaments.
source_questions.question: Assertion (A): Combining a 3D local intensity clustering-based level set that jointly estimates MRI bias fields with normal-vector intensity line profiling and variance-minimizing, thickness-constrained optimization enables automatic delineation of trabecular and cortical bone of the femur and tibia in proton density weighted knee MRI without training data and is robust to intensity inhomogeneity and weak edges near ligaments. Reason (R): This is because extensive training data are required to learn bone appearance.
source_questions.answer: A is True, R is False

mcq.question: Choose the correct option (A–D): The automatic bone delineation in proton density weighted knee MRI that remains robust to intensity inhomogeneity and weak edges near ligaments is best characterized as which paradigm regarding data requirements? [A] Unsupervised segmentation requiring no training data [B] Supervised segmentation requiring extensive labeled datasets [C] Semi-supervised segmentation dependent on partial labels [D] Interactive segmentation requiring user-drawn seeds

Choose the correct option (A–D): What directional sampling operation aids boundary localization when delineating bone in proton density weighted knee MRI? [A] Normal-vector intensity line profiling [B] Radial Hough voting [C] Phase-cycled averaging [D] K-space apodization
